ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for wholemeal bread with chocolate spread; mapped to broader bread food product class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — wholemeal bread with sweet spread (analogous estimate under FoodEx2 bread category)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for wholemeal bread with chocolate spread; A000E (bread and similar products) used as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite food item.

serving.description: 2 slices of wholemeal bread with chocolate spread
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving consists of two slices of wholemeal bread (approximately 60–70 g) spread with approximately 15–20 g of chocolate spread, totalling around 80 g.

nutrient_source.title: Wholemeal bread with chocolate spread — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database record F05020138 for wholemeal bread with chocolate spread. All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — wholemeal/whole wheat bread analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 69 (medium) is sourced from the HPB embedded GI database, consistent with published University of Sydney GI Database values for wholemeal bread (GI range 65–74). The composite GI reflects the dominant wholemeal bread component; chocolate spread contributes additional sugar but the bread matrix drives the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (69 × 44.53 × 0.80) / 100 ≈ 24.6 for an 80 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (13.21 g/100 g) with low potassium (74 mg/100 g) and moderate sodium (289 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: The sugar content of 13.21 g/100 g exceeds the solid-food sugar threshold of 10 g/100 g, which independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidance; potassium at 74 mg/100 g is insufficient to offset this effect, and individuals with hypertension should replace chocolate spread with unsweetened nut butter and pair with a high-potassium food such as banana or avocado.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (69), high sugar (13.21 g/100 g), and high glycemic load per serving (~24.6)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Despite 5.11 g/100 g dietary fibre from the wholemeal bread component, the chocolate spread contributes 13.21 g/100 g of sugar and drives a glycemic load of approximately 24.6 per serving, which is clinically high; individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit or avoid chocolate spread and substitute with a low-sugar, high-fibre topping such as unsweetened nut butter or avocado, and monitor post-meal blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Palm oil-based chocolate spread (pro-inflammatory saturated fat), high sugar (13.21 g/100 g), and moderate sodium (289 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Commercial chocolate spreads typically contain palm oil as the primary fat source, which is high in saturated fat and associated with adverse LDL cholesterol effects; combined with 13.21 g/100 g sugar and 289 mg/100 g sodium, this food presents a moderate cardiovascular risk and individuals with CVD should replace the chocolate spread with an unsaturated-fat alternative such as almond butter or avocado, while the wholemeal bread component provides beneficial dietary fibre (5.11 g/100 g) that partially offsets risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Palm oil in commercial chocolate spread is pro-inflammatory; sugar at 13.21 g/100 g approaches RA pro-inflammatory threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Commercial chocolate spreads containing palm oil are pro-inflammatory for rheumatoid arthritis; individuals with RA should replace chocolate spread with unsweetened cocoa mixed with extra-virgin olive oil or a small amount of 85%+ dark chocolate, and avoid regular consumption of this food.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wholemeal bread with chocolate spread is a popular breakfast and snack item in Singapore and internationally, consisting of sliced wholemeal (whole wheat) bread spread with a commercial chocolate spread such as Nutella or a similar hazelnut-cocoa product. The wholemeal bread provides a dense, slightly nutty texture and a mild wheaty flavour, while the chocolate spread adds a sweet, creamy, and rich chocolate-hazelnut taste. Per 100 g, this food delivers 293 kcal, 10.92 g protein, 8.38 g fat, 44.53 g carbohydrate, 13.21 g sugar, 5.11 g dietary fibre, and 289 mg sodium. The fibre content from the wholemeal bread is nutritionally beneficial, but the high sugar content from the chocolate spread (13.21 g/100 g) is a significant concern for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is 69 (medium), and the glycemic load per typical 80 g serving is approximately 24.6, which is clinically high. Commercial chocolate spreads typically contain palm oil as the primary fat, which is high in saturated fat and considered pro-inflammatory. Calcium is notably present at 165 mg/100 g and iron at 4.79 mg/100 g, likely from fortification of the wholemeal bread. This food is suitable for vegetarians and is generally halal-certified in Singapore, but contains gluten, tree nuts (hazelnut), and may contain soy and milk allergens. It is commonly consumed at breakfast or as a snack, and is widely available in Singapore households and cafes.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the canonical HPB Singapore Foodo database record F05020138 with confidence 0.95. GI value of 69 is consistent with published University of Sydney GI Database values for wholemeal bread. All core macros and key secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite food (wholemeal bread with chocolate spread) is not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database record F05020138; fatty acid sub-fractions are not available in the Singapore HPB database for this entry as of 2026. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo database for this record. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (69 × 44.53 × 0.80) / 100 ≈ 24.6 for an 80 g serving. The input record contained a caution note referencing 'sugar-sweetened beverage' which is inapplicable to this solid food; this has been corrected in the health context. All core macros (protein_g 10.92, fat_g 8.38, carbohydrate_g 44.53) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification required.
